log.why: Some protective spells intercept death events directly instead of reducing damage or applying healing.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Death Prevention Triggers. Purpose: Death Prevention Triggers exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Death Prevention Triggers`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].deathPrevention.triggers[]`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.drop_to_0_hp: Use when the spell changes a would-drop-to-0-HP event into a different result, such as dropping to 1 HP instead.
acceptedValueNotes.instant_death_no_damage: Use when the spell negates an effect that would kill the target instantly without dealing damage.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use only when canonical prose has no death-prevention mechanic.

fields.structuredLabel: Death Prevention Drop To Hit Points

log.why: A 0-HP prevention rule needs the replacement hit point total instead of hiding it in description text.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Death Prevention Drop To Hit Points. Purpose: Death Prevention Drop To Hit Points exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Death Prevention Drop To Hit Points`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].deathPrevention.dropToHitPoints`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.number: Use the exact replacement hit point total from canonical prose.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no replacement hit point total applies.

fields.structuredLabel: Death Prevention Negates Instant Death Without Damage

log.why: Instant-kill prevention is not the same as damage resistance, immunity, or healing.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Death Prevention Negates Instant Death Without Damage. Purpose: Death Prevention Negates Instant Death Without Damage exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Death Prevention Negates Instant Death Without Damage`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].deathPrevention.negatesInstantDeathWithoutDamage`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.true: Use when canonical prose explicitly negates instant death without damage.
acceptedValueNotes.false: Use when a death-prevention mechanic exists but does not negate instant death without damage.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell has no death-prevention mechanic.

log.why: The runtime needs to know whether a death-prevention ward remains active after it fires.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Death Prevention Consumption. Purpose: Death Prevention Consumption exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Death Prevention Consumption`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].deathPrevention.consumption`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.first_trigger_ends_spell: Use when the first qualifying death-prevention event consumes or ends the spell.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell has no death-prevention mechanic.

log.why: The runtime needs to know who receives the death-prevention benefit.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Death Prevention Scope. Purpose: Death Prevention Scope exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Death Prevention Scope`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].deathPrevention.scope`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.target: Use when the spell's target receives the death-prevention benefit.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell has no death-prevention mechanic.

log.why: Some spells remove spell-created state when the spell or effect ends without creating a separate early-ending trigger.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Trigger. Purpose: End Cleanup Trigger exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this cleanup mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Trigger`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].trigger`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.spell_ends: Use when cleanup runs because the whole spell ends.
acceptedValueNotes.effect_ends: Use when cleanup runs because one effect ends while other spell parts may remain.
acceptedValueNotes.item_effect_ends: Use when cleanup belongs to a spell-created or spell-empowered item effect ending.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use only when canonical prose has no explicit end-cleanup mechanic.

log.why: The runtime needs to know which spell-created state is removed at cleanup time.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Removes. Purpose: End Cleanup Removes exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this cleanup mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Removes`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].removes`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.temporary_hit_points: Use when cleanup removes Temporary Hit Points created by the spell or effect.
acceptedValueNotes.spell_granted_flying_speed: Use when cleanup removes a flying speed granted by this spell or one of its effects.
acceptedValueNotes.extradimensional_space: Use when cleanup removes a spell-created extradimensional space or pocket.
acceptedValueNotes.created_ammunition: Use when cleanup removes or destroys ammunition created by this spell.
acceptedValueNotes.spell_material_container: Use when cleanup destroys or removes a material container that the spell uses as an ongoing anchor.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no end-cleanup state is removed.

log.why: Temporary Hit Points and other states can come from several sources, so cleanup must name the source it owns.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Source. Purpose: End Cleanup Source exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this cleanup mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Source`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].source`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.this_spell: Use when cleanup removes state granted by this spell as a whole.
acceptedValueNotes.this_effect: Use when cleanup removes state granted only by the same effect packet.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no end-cleanup source applies.

log.why: The runtime needs to know who loses the spell-created state during cleanup.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Scope. Purpose: End Cleanup Scope exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this cleanup mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Scope`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].scope`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.target: Use when the spell target loses the spell-created state.
acceptedValueNotes.caster: Use when the caster loses the spell-created state.
acceptedValueNotes.affected_creatures: Use when all currently affected creatures lose the spell-created state.
acceptedValueNotes.contents: Use when cleanup affects the creatures, objects, or other contents inside a spell-created space, container, or passage.
acceptedValueNotes.created_objects: Use when cleanup affects objects created by the spell rather than creatures or contents.
acceptedValueNotes.spell_component: Use when cleanup affects the material component or container that anchors the spell.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no end-cleanup scope applies.

log.why: Cleanup can remove all remaining spell-created state or a narrower amount in future spells.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Amount. Purpose: End Cleanup Amount exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this cleanup mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Amount`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].amount`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.all_remaining: Use when all remaining matching state from the named source is removed.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no end-cleanup amount applies.

log.why: Some cleanup rules immediately create a follow-up runtime consequence instead of only removing state.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Consequence. Purpose: End Cleanup Consequence exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about cleanup consequences. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Consequence`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].consequence`. Use `not_applicable` only when cleanup has no follow-up consequence.

acceptedValueNotes.fall_if_aloft: Use when the affected creature falls after losing spell-granted flight if it is still aloft.
acceptedValueNotes.contents_drop_out: Use when contents inside a spell-created space or container drop out when that space or container ends.
acceptedValueNotes.disintegrate: Use when cleanup causes spell-created objects or ammunition to disintegrate.
acceptedValueNotes.destroy: Use when cleanup destroys a spell-owned object or material container.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no cleanup consequence applies.

log.why: The runtime needs to know when a cleanup consequence is conditional rather than automatic.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Prevented By. Purpose: End Cleanup Prevented By exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about exceptions to cleanup consequences. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Prevented By`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].preventedBy`. Use `not_applicable` only when the cleanup consequence has no prevention clause.

acceptedValueNotes.can_prevent_fall: Use when another ability, spell, trait, or other runtime state can stop the fall after spell-granted flight ends.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no prevention clause applies.

log.why: Some cleanup consequences move contents out of a spell-created space and need a destination that is narrower than a full forced-placement system.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured End Cleanup Destination. Purpose: End Cleanup Destination exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about where cleanup moves contents. Populate it from the structured markdown field `End Cleanup Destination`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].endCleanup[].destination`. Use `not_applicable` only when cleanup does not move or place contents.

acceptedValueNotes.space_exit_anchor: Use when contents leave through, or drop out at, the spell-created space's entrance, exit, or anchor point.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no cleanup destination applies.

log.why: Some spells give the target or another subject a new action instead of giving the caster a repeat action.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Actor. Purpose: Granted Action Actor exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Actor`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].actor`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.caster: Use when the caster can use the granted action.
acceptedValueNotes.target: Use when the spell target can use the granted action.
acceptedValueNotes.summoned_entity: Use when a summoned or created entity can use the granted action.
acceptedValueNotes.affected_creature: Use when any currently affected creature can use the granted action.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not grant an action.

log.why: The runtime needs a stable action identifier rather than relying only on prose.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Name. Purpose: Granted Action Name exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Name`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].action`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not grant an action.

log.why: A spell-granted Magic action is mechanically different from a generic action label.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Type. Purpose: Granted Action Type exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Type`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].actionKind`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.magic_action: Use when canonical prose says the granted action is a Magic action.
acceptedValueNotes.standard_action: Use when the granted action is an ordinary action without the Magic-action tag.
acceptedValueNotes.bonus_action: Use when the granted action costs a Bonus Action.
acceptedValueNotes.reaction: Use when the granted action costs a Reaction.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not grant an action.

log.why: The runtime needs to know whether the granted action is repeatable while the spell remains active.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Frequency. Purpose: Granted Action Frequency exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Frequency`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].frequency`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.once: Use when the granted action can be used one time.
acceptedValueNotes.each_turn: Use when the granted action can be used on each eligible turn.
acceptedValueNotes.while_active: Use when the action remains generally available while the spell is active.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell does not grant an action.

log.why: Some granted actions emit a shaped area, such as Dragon's Breath creating a cone.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Area Shape. Purpose: Granted Action Area Shape exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Area Shape`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].areaShape`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.Cone: Use when the granted action creates a cone.
acceptedValueNotes.Line: Use when the granted action creates a line.
acceptedValueNotes.Sphere: Use when the granted action creates a sphere.
acceptedValueNotes.Cube: Use when the granted action creates a cube.
acceptedValueNotes.Cylinder: Use when the granted action creates a cylinder.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the granted action has no area shape.

log.why: A granted area action needs its numeric area size outside prose.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Area Size. Purpose: Granted Action Area Size exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Area Size`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].areaSize`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the granted action has no area size.

log.why: Area size needs an explicit unit for runtime use.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Area Size Unit. Purpose: Granted Action Area Size Unit exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Area Size Unit`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].areaSizeUnit`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.feet: Use when the granted action area is measured in feet.
acceptedValueNotes.miles: Use when the granted action area is measured in miles.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the granted action has no area size unit.

log.why: The granted action should point to the runtime effect packets it activates.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Effect Indices. Purpose: Granted Action Effect Indices exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about this granted-action mechanic. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Effect Indices`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].effectIndices[]`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when there are no linked effect indexes.

log.why: Some granted actions can only be used while a stated condition remains true.

fields.comment: Mapped from structured Granted Action Prerequisites. Purpose: Granted Action Prerequisites exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about repeat-action restrictions. Populate it from the structured markdown field `Granted Action Prerequisites`, after that field has been checked against canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].grantedActions[].prerequisites[]`. Use `not_applicable` only when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.target_object_within_spell_range: Use when the granted action can only be used while the original target object remains within the spell's range.
acceptedValueNotes.target_within_spell_range: Use when the granted action can only be used while the original creature or target remains within the spell's range.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the granted action has no extra prerequisite or the spell grants no action.
